method_atom: A construction of local, symmetry-preserving operators (a sum of projector-like local terms) whose null space is confined to a combinatorial basis of pairwise-pairing (dimer/matching) configurations on a graph. The approach proves the linear independence of these pairing configurations on arbitrarily large graphs, and uses that independence to establish that the resulting degenerate null states form a small, topologically distinguished family that is unique within the constrained subspace (and, under stated conditions, within the full state space).
object_atom: SU(2)-invariant spin-1/2 quantum Hamiltonians on the kagome lattice, whose ground states lie in the space of nearest-neighbor valence-bond (singlet-pair) states, including generalized Klein models and a resonating-valence-bond model with four topologically degenerate Sutherland-Rokhsar-Kivelson wavefunctions describing a gapped Z2 spin liquid.
brief_reason: The transferable machinery is the linear-independence proof for pairwise-pairing configurations on a graph plus the construction of local operators whose kernel yields a topologically degenerate, uniquely determined ground-state family; the domain-specific object is the spin-1/2 kagome-lattice valence-bond system and its Z2 spin liquid.
